title: Motivation
text:
Thank you so much for exploring my webpage! On this tap, I will show you my motivations for this project.



This is my niece. Her name is Tina.
What you should know about her:

Strength: Has nice hair,brows and lashes
Weakness: Always has a mission to ask 15 question per minute, especially the “Auntie, have you eaten?” question



This is my nephew. His name is Mesu.
Here is what you should know about him:

Strength: Has nice teeth
Weakness: calls me at 2am and then complains to my mom by saying that I never pick up his call




Have a nice day!

title: Map
text:
The states dataset in the poliscidata package contains 135 variables on each of the 50 US states. See here for more detail.
In this tab, I will create a two meaningful choropleth plots, one using a numeric variable and one using a categorical variable from poliscidata::states. You will see two versions of each plot: a static plot using the maps package and ggplot(), and an interactive plot using the sf package and leaflet(). Finally, I will write a sentence or two describing what I learn from each plot.

Numeric variable (static plot):









Motor vehicle fatality rates vary significantly across states, with higher rates concentrated in the South and some Mountain West states. In contrast, states in the Northeast and on the West Coast tend to have lower fatality rates. This pattern may reflect differences in population density, road conditions, and driving behavior, as rural areas often experience higher speeds and longer emergency response times.


Numeric variable (interactive plot):






The interactive feature allows users to hover over each state to view precise fatality rates, providing more detailed insight than the static map.


Categorical variable (static plot)









This map shows which political party each state tends to support. The pattern shows that many coastal states lean Democratic, while many states in the South, Midwest, and Mountain West lean Republican.


Categorical variable (interactive plot):






By hovering over the states, users can see the exact category for each state, making it easier to compare regional political patterns across the country.

title: Welcome!
text:
Hey! this is Sokmean
Thank you for stopping by! I was born and raised in Cambodia. I moved to Singapore in 2019 for school. Right now, I’m a student at St. Olaf College. I’m doing Quantitative Economics and Statistics doubled majors. This is my first time building a website and I will display my work in data science 2.
Fun Facts About Me

Data Science only starts to make sense to me at 2am
I shop every time I submit a data science’s project because I truly deserve it ( this one too)
My lashes will be on whenever I’m stressed, the longer the lashes, the harder time I’m going through. Sometimes I’m so close to fly with them too
I love love, and you should too
